response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/C/C++ exposure, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization at a Principal level. The resume shows no evidence of hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD architecture work, DNN training/inference optimization, or chip-level performance analysis. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and embedded mentions do not approach the specialization required for a Principal Performance Architect on AI accelerators.
strengths: Broad senior engineering experience across multiple languages and stacks
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems work (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services)
Prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No demonstrated AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or DNN optimization background
Background is application/web software, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator / DNN / GPU performance optimization background
Senior IC experience with some low-latency systems work
